Sheer Force avoids Life Orb chip damage and boosts Thundurus's power, allowing it to 2HKO Deoxys-D and max HP Scream Tail after Stealth Rock, while Magic Guard also avoids Life Orb damage and chip damage from Stealth Rock. Thundurus struggles to sweep opposing teams possessing fast revenge killers such as Roaring Moon, Azelf, and Choice Scarf Gholdengo; therefore, it requires allies like Corviknight and Empoleon that easily switch into these threats and pivot it in. Fast special attacking pivots such as Azelf and Iron Moth appreciate Thundurus's ability to wallbreak, as it allows them to clean more easily.

Bolstering an incredible Special Attack stat combined with a great movepool makes Thundurus-T an extremely threatening wallbreaker. Focus Blast nails foes such as Hisuian Goodra, Volt Absorb Empoleon, and Volt Absorb Corviknight. Psychic hits Great Tusk and Iron Hands, while Grass Knot can let Thundurus-T lure in and take out Swampert while still hitting Great Tusk for super effective damage. Nasty Plot boosts Thundurus-T's Special Attack to nuclear levels, allowing it to OHKO Hisuian Goodra and Blissey with Focus Blast after slight chip damage. Fast specially offensive pivots such as Iron Moth, Choice Scarf Azelf, and Latios appreciate Thundurus-T's incredible wallbreaking power, as they can take advantage of weakened special walls such as Swampert, Manaphy, and Meloetta. Thundurus-T loves slow, defensive pivots such as Corviknight, physically defensive Manaphy, and Swampert, as they can take on faster threats such as Iron Boulder, Chien-Pao, and Azelf, which all threaten to KO it, while also being able to safely pivot it in. Entry hazard Removal from the likes of Corviknight, Great Tusk, and Mandibuzz is also helpful, as Thundurus-T is very vulnerable to Stealth Rock. Sticky Web support from a teammate like Smeargle or Ribombee is another option, as it can give Thundurus-T the opportunity to outpace previously mentioned revenge killers.

Desolate Land.description: Landorus is a good wallbreaker, taking advantage of Desolate Land's Water-type protection, Weather Ball's type synergy, and its Ground-type STAB moves to blast through Pokemon such as Corviknight, Iron Moth, and Gholdengo. Earth Power is used for its consistency, while Sandsear Storm provides higher power, allowing it to have a chance to OHKO Jirachi and Pixilate Scream Tail after a Nasty Plot; additionally, Sandsear Storm has the ability to burn foes, which can help if it doesn't take the KO. Grass Knot helps Landorus take on bulky Ground-types such as Swampert and Great Tusk, while Stealth Rock lets Landorus support its teammates, weaken its own checks like Primarina and non-Heavy-Duty Boots Dragonite, and threaten common entry hazard removal options such as Corviknight and the aforementioned Great Tusk. Using Leftovers lets Landorus stay on the field longer, while Life Orb lets it OHKO Swampert and 2HKO Primarina with a +2 Grass Knot. Pokemon like Iron Moth, Azelf, and Deoxys-S appreciate Landorus for being able to take on checks like Swampert, Empoleon, and Iron Treads. Landorus struggles to deal with Pokemon that aren't weak to its moves like Manaphy, Blissey, and RegenVest Roaring Moon, so having teammates like Latios, Great Tusk, and Iron Hands will help with taking them down. Similarly, Landorus enjoys having teammates that can remove items with Knock Off such as Roaring Moon, Manaphy, and Azelf, helping it to break down RegenVest Pokemon like Meloetta and Manaphy as well as Pokemon with Heavy-Duty Boots like Dragonite and Mandibuzz. Landorus struggles to switch in on many Pokemon due to its frailty, so it appreciates slow pivots such as Corviknight and Primarina, which also help it by checking offensive Pokemon like Roaring Moon, Iron Boulder, and Walking Wake.

Landorus-Therian.comments:
Other Options

Landorus-T can utilize Desolate Land to both act as a switch-in to Primordial Sea users like Zapdos and Primarina and threaten Steel-types like Corviknight and Skarmory with Weather Ball, although it would be forgoing its pivoting capabilities. Landorus-T can also be used as a fully offensive wallbreaker with Mold Breaker or Sword of Ruin in conjunction with STAB Earthquake and solid coverage options. Well-Baked Body can also be utilized let it act as a defensive pivot against Fire-types like Iron Moth, Moltres, and Heatran.

Checks and Counters

Special Attackers: Strong special attackers like Latios, Gholdengo, and Deoxys-S all OHKO Landorus-T.

Ice-type Coverage: Ice-type moves from Iron Hands, Chien-Pao, and Great Tusk can seriously threaten Landorus-T.

Bulky Water-types: RegenVest Water-types such as Primarina, Swampert, and Manaphy can stomach hits from Landorus-T while also threatening it with Water-type moves or Knock Off in return.

Fluffy.description:
With a solid defensive typing and physical bulk with Fluffy, Landorus-T functions as a physically defensive pivot against some of the tier's most threatening physical attackers in Zamazenta, Great Tusk, and Roaring Moon while also having offensive stats to damage many Pokemon in return. Smack Down threatens Flying-types like Corviknight, Zapdos, and Skarmory as well as common Levitate users such as Gholdengo and Tinkaton, while Taunt can prevent Defog and Roost from the aforementioned Corviknight or setup of entry hazards from Pokemon like Great Tusk and Ting-Lu. Rocky Helmet provides chip damage against the strong physical attackers Landorus-T is able to check like Zamazenta as well as Corviknight and Great Tusk, which may be looking to use U-turn or Rapid Spin, respectively. Leftovers can also be used to provide longevity, allowing it to withstand more hits from physical attackers and mitigating the chip damage from Stealth Rock. 8 Speed EVs allow Landorus-T to outspeed Choice Specs Primarina and Hisuian Goodra.

Landorus-T is used mostly on offensive and balanced teams as role compression for both an entry hazard setter and a defensive pivot, being one of the few Pokemon with access to both Stealth Rock and U-turn. Offensive Dark-types like Roaring Moon, Kingambit, and Chien-Pao appreciate Landorus-T's ability to pivot in on Zamazenta and its ability to threaten Corviknight as well. Special attackers like Gholdengo, Primordial Sea Zapdos, and Latios can threaten Landorus-T, so specially defensive pivots like RegenVest Swampert and Iron Hands complement Landorus-T well. Landorus-T can be threatened by Ice-type coverage from both Chien-Pao and Iron Hands, which makes Steel-types like Gholdengo and Kingambit potential allies to alleviate the issue. In return, Landorus-T provides a natural Ground immunity and Fighting resistance for them. Landorus-T appreciates hazard removal, so Pokemon ike Iron Treads, Great Tusk, and Corviknight make for nice teammates. Due to Fluffy, Landorus-T can be susceptible to opposing Fire-types like Cinderace and Ogerpon-H; thus, it values Fire-immune allies like Well-Baked Body Gholdengo and Primordial Sea Zapdos.

RegenVest.description: RegenVest Meloetta is a capable pivot thanks to its ability to switch into most special attackers like Gholdengo, Scream Tail, Deoxys-S, and Iron Moth. Knock Off gives Meloetta an option to support its teammates by removing key items such as Assault Vest from Swampert and Hisuian Goodra, while Thunderbolt hits Manaphy and non-Volt Absorb Corviknight for high damage. Low Kick lets Meloetta chip Hisuian Goodra and Heatran, but Energy Ball can be used instead to 2HKO Swampert after its Assault Vest has been removed. Good teammates include Pokemon like Roaring Moon, Galarian Zapdos, Barraskewda, and Chien-Pao because Meloetta can safely get them into the field with a slow U-turn. Physical attackers like Roaring Moon and Chien-Pao naturally threaten Meloetta out, so Pokemon that can deal with them, like Corviknight, Great Tusk, and defensive Quaquaval, make for good partners. Lastly, Meloetta appreciates entry hazard removal from the likes of Corviknight, Great Tusk, and Mandibuzz, to keep its HP high enough to switch into strong attackers.

Flame Body.description: Chesnaught's bulk, recovery, and useful resistances let it frustrate several common physical attackers, most notably Roaring Moon, Ogerpon-C, and Barraskewda, and Spikes provides utility in nearly every matchup. Flame Body has a 30% chance to end the usefulness of opposing offensive pivots, again, most notably Roaring Moon, and greatly frustrates Rapid Spin Great Tusk attempting to clear Spikes. Iron Defense is the preferred fourth move to set up alongside Bulk Up Great Tusk and Swords Dance users like Iron Hands and Garchomp, while Knock Off can help remove Heavy-Duty Boots to further accentuate Spikes. The given EV spread lets Chesnaught outpace Corviknight, giving it extra chances to fish for a burn, as Brave Bird will not 2HKO it after Iron Defense. Chesnaught mostly finds its home on stall teams, where it can find plenty of teammates to switch into its common weaknesses, especially Fire and Psychic, and can find multiple switch-in opportunities to try and activate Flame Body, which is one of the few ways for stall teams to punish pivoting. Chesnaught is especially horrified of Fire-types like Ceruledge and Cinderace, as they can obliterate it without fear of a burn, and it cannot touch Ghost-types like Gengar and Hisuian Zoroark without Knock Off, so teammates like Well-Baked Body Mandibuzz are strongly recommended.

Magic Guard.description: Despite its low Attack stat, Talonflame carves out a niche as a usable wallbreaker and potent stallbreaker. Roost keeps Talonflame healthy throughout the course of a game, allowing it to reliably act as an offensive check to threats like Ogerpon-H, Great Tusk, and Iron Moth. Bulk Up gives revenge killers like Dragonite and Choice Scarf Roaring Moon a harder time at attempting to revenge kill Talonflame while threatening to let it snowball out of control. Flare Blitz smacks Steel-types like Corviknight, Gholdengo, and Iron Treads, while Swords Dance provides greater damage potential, allowing Talonflame to threaten a 2HKO on Pecharunt and OHKO Dragonite from full HP. Taunt allows Talonflame to reliably act as a stallbreaker, preventing would-be checks like Well-Baked Body Corviknight, Unaware Scream Tail, and Deoxys-D from checking it, while Will-O-Wisp provides more utility, burning switch-ins like Garganacl, Primordial Sea Zapdos, and Archaludon. Talonflame requires support from Pokemon that can wear the opposing team down, so entry hazard setters like Great Tusk, Skarmory, and Empoleon are wonderful teammates for it. Talonflame's unimpressive bulk necessitates good teammates that can switch into revenge killers like Azelf, Barraskewda, and Hisuian Electrode; hence, the aforementioned hazard setters, Iron Moth, and Blissey all work as excellent partners for it. Talonflame also notably requires teammates such as RegenVest Swampert and Meloetta that can deal with Fire-immune Electric- and Steel-types like Well-Baked Body Gholdengo, Primordial Sea Zapdos, and Archaludon.

Primordial Sea.description: Primordial Sea Volcanion excels as a fantastic wallbreaker with its great Special Attack and offensive typing. Rain-boosted Steam Eruption combined with Choice Specs allows Volcanion to 2HKO most Pokemon in tier, including most specially defensive walls such as RegenVest Garchomp, Meloetta, and Alolan Muk. Weather Ball dissuades promient Desolate Land users that might want to switch in to Volcanion such as Cinderace and Iron Moth. Earth Power is a more reliable way to deal with Desolate Land users like Iron Moth and Heatran, and it's a reliable way to hit Hisuian Goodra without missing. Focus Blast 2HKOes a would-be check in RegenVest Roaring Moon, as well as Hisuian Goodra after Spikes chip. Volcanion pairs well with entry hazard setters like Ting-Lu and Garchomp, which help wear down common RegenVest Pokemon like Goodra and Hisuian Goodra, and hazards also guarantee a 2HKO on Meloetta. Due to Volcanion's Stealth Rock weakness it pairs well with hazard removers like Corviknight and Quaquaval, which can also help pivot it in safely. Volcanion also pairs well with offensive pivots like Zapdos and Roaring Moon, which can help bring it in on targets like Corviknight.

Primarina.comments:
Other Options

On RegenVest sets, Psychic can be used instead of Psychic Noise for a chance to 2HKO Iron Moth but is generally less useful due to the latter's denial of HP recovery. Hydro Pump can also be considered for more immediate damage on RegenVest sets in place of Energy Ball or Surf. Primarina does have a few niche sets, mainly a physically defensive set with the Stamina ability and Leftovers. This set aims to rack up stat boosts through its ability and the move Calm Mind to sweep with the move Stored Power.

Checks and Counters

Electric Types: Electric-types like Zapdos and Sandy Shocks force out Primarina with their strong attacks, often 2HKOing RegenVest sets and OHKOing offensive Primarina.

Desolate Land Users: Desolate Land users such as Heatran and Moltres can deny Primarina's Flip Turn and take all of Primarina's attacks relatively easily.

Grass-Types: Primarina is threatened by several Grass-types that are staples on teams in the tier. On the physical side, it is threatened by Power Whip from the Ogerpon formes and Flower Trick from Sword of Ruin Meowscarada. On the special side, Sinistcha threatens Primarina with Matcha Gotcha and can often KO it after a Calm Mind boost.

Choice Specs.description:
Primarina is one of the strongest special wallbreakers in the tier, with an excellent typing that gives it opportunities to switch in and spam its powerful and reliable STAB moves. Primordial Sea boosts Weather Ball and lets Primarina check Desolate Land users such as Moltres, non-Discharge Iron Moth, and non-Gunk Shot Cinderace. However, these same threats are also great checks to Primarina if they negate Primordial Sea, so Psychic is helpful as the strongest option against them, particularly Iron Moth, while Psychic Noise is a weaker alternative that prevents recovery moves from Moltres and Iron Moth. Flip Turn lets Primarina pivot on special walls such as Manaphy and Bulletproof Iron Treads, generating momentum for its team.

Primarina fits well on balance and bulky offense teams, where its mix of defensive utility and offensive pressure is put to good use. It resists the STAB moves of many common Choice item wallbreakers such as Chien-Pao and Zamazenta, though it still needs backup from physical walls like Corviknight and Pecharunt, especially against the Ogerpon formes. Great Tusk and Corviknight can also provide entry hazard removal, letting Primarina stay healthy so it can threaten out the aforementioned Choice Band wallbreakers. Faster special attackers such as Zapdos, Latios, and Deoxys-S are also problematic, so special walls such as Iron Treads and Swampert are good teammates. In exchange, Primarina can deter Fire-type moves that threaten Corviknight and Iron Treads. Primarina struggles against special walls like Manaphy and the aforementioned Desolate Land users, especially specially defensive Moltres, so it appreciates physical attackers like the Ogerpon formes and Roaring Moon.

RegenVest.description:
An excellent type combination, good natural bulk and access to Flip Turn allow Primarina to function as a solid RegenVest user . Psychic Noise hits Desolate Land Iron Moth and Moltres for a good chunk of damage and denies recovery as well. Energy Ball is useful against Swampert, Manaphy, and opposing Primarina. This moveslot may also be swapped for Surf or Hydro Pump to provide a second powerful STAB option for more reliable neutral damage against Corviknight and Skarmory. This set runs a Speed-reducing nature and 0 Speed IVs to ensure that Primarina is the slowest Pokemon on the field and can safely pivot to bring in teammates using Flip Turn.

RegenVest Primarina performs well as a bulky pivot on offensive archetypes due to its excellent mixed defensive profile, checking tier staples both on the physical and special side. Although it cannot learn Knock Off, unlike other RegenVest Water types, Primarina is able to counter the likes of Roaring Moon, Chien-Pao, Great Tusk, and Zamazenta with its Fairy typing, as well as handling specially offensive threats such as Gholdengo and Latios quite well. This set must be wary of potential Knock Off users as its ability to take special hits is crippled if it loses its Assault Vest. Therefore, Primarina appreciates support from teammates like Great Tusk and Corviknight to absorb Knock Off; they can also aid Primarina with entry hazard removal. Strong Electric-type attackers like offensive Iron Hands, Zapdos, and Sandy Shocks can pressure and break through Primarina, so it could use Electric-immune teammates like Great Tusk, Iron Treads and Volt Absorb Corviknight. Desolate Land users such as Heatran or Moltres can deny Primarina Flip Turn pivots and immediately threaten it with Solar Beam. Primordial Sea Zapdos or Well-Baked Body Gholdengo can switch into these threats and give Primarina safe entry.

Physical Wall.description: Toxapex leverages its high defensive stats and plethora of useful utility moves to act as a stout defensive wall and disruptor. Prankster + Haze lets Toxapex shut down threatening setup sweepers such as Ogerpon-Wellspring, Manaphy, and Stored Power Iron Crown. Corrosion + Toxic gives Toxapex the ability to punish Steel-types such as Corviknight, Gholdengo, and Desolate Land Heatran, which can otherwise shrug off Toxapex's attacks, while Toxic Spikes can pressure more offensively oriented teams lacking their own Poison-type. Chilling Water deters physical attackers such as Iron Hands and Great Tusk from switching into Toxapex, while Infestation can rack up chip damage on bulkier foes such as Corviknight and Empoleon. Black Sludge give Toxapex greater longevity, while Rocky Helmet punishes physical attackers such as Chien-Pao and Barraskewda, as well as general U-turn spam. Toxapex fits best on stall teams due to its passivity and ability to check setup sweepers that could potentially run rampant. Certain wallbreakers such as Iron Boulder, Garchomp, and Iron Hands are able to power through Toxapex, so teammates that can check these such as Great Tusk and Corviknight are greatly appreciated; Toxapex can in return take on Chien-Pao and Fire-types such as Cinderace and Ogerpon-H. Toxapex is shocked by Electric- and Psychic-types such as Zapdos, Deoxys-S, and Azelf, so teammates that can reliably take these on such as Hisuian Goodra, Mandibuzz, and Meloetta are helpful.

Galvanize.description: Kommo-o finds a decent niche on offensive teams as a mixed attacker with strong coverage. Boomburst scares off walls such as Mandibuzz, Corviknight, and Manaphy. Clanging Scales threatens Great Tusk, Iron Hands, and Pecharunt, while Close Combat notably allows Kommo-o to smack special walls like Blissey, Swampert, and Volt Absorb Empoleon attempting to check it. Stealth Rock synergizes well with Kommo-o's ability to scare away entry hazard removers like the aforementioned Mandibuzz and Corviknight. Clangorous Soul allows it to act as a passable setup sweeper instead, preventing threats like Roaring Moon and Iron Boulder from revenge killing it while increasing its offensive capabilities. Life Orb is typically preferred for its consistent damage boost, but Throat Spray can be used on Clangorous Soul sets to avoid recoil. Kommo-o's lack of longevity necessitates either teammates that can make up for its absent defensive utility like Corviknight and Manaphy or offensive teammates that can keep up the pressure like Iron Moth and Chien-Pao. Select revenge killers like Dragonite, Choice Scarf Roaring Moon, and Azelf naturally outspeed Kommo-o even after a Clangorous Soul, so answers to them like Kingambit, Great Tusk, and Roaring Moon make for good partners. Kommo-o appreciates pivots like Azelf, Sandy Shocks, and Roaring Moon because they can pivot out on their checks like Blissey, Empoleon, and Corviknight and safely bring it in to start threatening the opposing team. Kommo-o greatly appreciates dual screens support from setters like Corviknight and Deoxys-S allowing it to have an easier time setting up with Clangorous Soul or supporting its teammates with Stealth Rock; alternatively, Sticky Web from Smeargle and Ribombee can help it outspeed Choice Scarf revenge killers like Latios, Azelf, and Roaring Moon after a Clangorous Soul.

Cinderace.comments:
Other Options

Cinderace can run Choice Band and have strong results, with its great Speed allowing it to pressure slower Pokemon and significantly dent its checks attempting to switch in on it; the main downside to Choice Band is that Cinderace is put on a strict timer due to its Stealth Rock weakness. Cinderace can also use Mold Breaker as a way to get around its biggest adversary, Well-Baked Body, but it loses a lot of power due to not having Desolate Land's sun. This also guts any opportunities Cinderace would have against Primordial Sea users. Lastly, in combination with Taunt, Super Fang gives Cinderace a potent way to break down tougher walls; however, this pairing leaves Cinderace with fewer options against frailer teams that appreciate their Pokemon never being knocked out by Super Fang. There are also many ways around Super Fang, namely Regenerator and Ghost-type Pokemon.

Checks and Counters

Fire-immune Pokemon: Cinderace is unable to make much progress when facing common Fire-immune Pokemon like Well-Baked Body Gholdengo and Iron Treads as well as Primordial Sea Zapdos.

Primarina: Primarina is able to tank any attack Cinderace can throw at it while either healing with Regenerator or threatening Cinderace out with Primordial Sea.

Court Change.description:
Desolate Land allows Cinderace to offensively check Water-type attackers like Ogerpon-W and Primarina while also boosting the power of its Fire-type moves, namely Pyro Ball. Meanwhile, Magic Guard sacrifices some of Cinderace's firepower compared to Desolate Land but allows it to use Life Orb; avoid taking damage from entry hazards; and take advantage of the more reliable Flare Blitz, which can notably threaten Bulletproof Pokemon like Iron Treads and Gholdengo at the cost of dealing less damage than Pyro Ball to Fluffy users. U-turn allows for Cinderace to play a fast pivot role, being able to bait in and switch out on its checks like Pecharunt and Great Tusk, while Swords Dance provides it the ability to pressure its checks with serious damage or act as a late-game cleaner aided by its great Speed tier. Court Change lets Cinderace act as an extra form of entry hazard control for its team, allowing it to take advantage of hazard stacking or Sticky Web teams by flipping the hazards back to the other team. Will-O-Wisp grants it a way to cripple any non-Well-Baked Body Pokemon attempting to take it on. On the other hand, Taunt aids it in breaking down tough walls by preventing them from healing, with the exception of Regenerator Pokemon, and prevents Defog users from clearing any hazards switched onto their side with Court Change. High Jump Kick is another option that lets Cinderace threaten Well-Baked Body Pokemon like Cobalion and Iron Treads.

Cinderace pairs well with attackers such as Chien-Pao, Deoxys-S, and Roaring Moon, providing all of them easy entry points through its entry hazard control and pivoting while also weakening or beating their checks like Cobalion, Ting-Lu, and Levitate Gholdengo. With Court Change, Cinderace provides unique support to offensive teams, not only removing a major hindrance to offensive staples like Deoxys-S, Zamazenta, and all-out attacker Iron Moth with its ability to move Sticky Web off of its side, but also helping these Pokemon by inflicting Sticky Web onto the opposing team. Cinderace mostly struggles to deal with Fire-immune Pokemon like Well-Baked Body Landorus-T as well as Primordial Sea Zapdos and Primarina, so having teammates like Zapdos, Roaring Moon, and Hisuian Goodra will help it out immensely.

Primordial Sea.description: Inteleon is a decent cleaner and wallbreaker thanks to its high Special Attack and Speed, threatening many offensive Pokemon like Great Tusk, Scream Tail, and Gholdengo. U-turn lets it pivot out of unfavorable matchups like RegenVest Manaphy and Primarina and forms a VoltTurn core with other pivots like Roaring Moon and Meowscarada. Mud Shot threatens Fire-types like Iron Moth and Cinderace that could otherwise utilize Desolate Land to comfortably switch into a Weather Ball and OHKOes Heatran. Ice Beam snipes Dragon-types like Roaring Moon and Latios. Physical teammates like Zamazenta and Roaring Moon appreciate Inteleon's ability to threaten their switch-ins like Pecharunt and Great Tusk, respectively, letting them rip massive holes in the opponent's team once these foes are removed; in return, they can threaten walls like Empoleon and Manaphy, which Inteleon struggles against. Entry hazard support from the likes of Scream Tail and Ting-Lu helps Inteleon against special walls like Empoleon and RegenVest users like Manaphy and Primarina, which it otherwise struggles to break through. Inteleon is also vulnerable to being worn down by hazards, so teammates that can remove them like Corviknight and Great Tusk are good partners. Furthermore, Inteleon's frailty leaves it severely threatened by faster revenge killers like Chien-Pao and Choice Scarf Roaring Moon, making teammates that can deal with them like the aforementioned Corviknight and Great Tusk greatly appreciated.

Corviknight.comments:
Other Options

Physically Defensive Corviknight sets can use Well-Baked Body to check a wide variety of Fire-types, including Cinderace, Ogerpon-H, Moltres, Heatran, and Iron Moth lacking Discharge; however, it loses a lot of bulk and results in worse matchups against Roaring Moon, Zamazenta, and offensive Great Tusk. Specially defensive sets can use Water Absorb to check Ogerpon-W, Manaphy, and Choice Specs Primarina while preventing Swampert and Primarina from pivoting out with Flip Turn or Bulletproof to let Corviknight check Cinderace, Moltres, Choice Specs Primarina, and Gholdengo lacking Thunderbolt, but Corviknight often gets getter value out of its other abilities. Corviknight can run a set with Bulk Up, Agility, Roost, and Power Trip with an immunity ability such as Volt Absorb on hyper offense teams to surprise opponents and potentially sweep teams if lacking appropriate checks.

Checks and Counters

Electric-type Moves: Without Volt Absorb, Corviknight gets destroyed by Electric-types like Zapdos and Sandy Shocks as well as Electric-type move coverage like Thunderbolt Latios and Deoxys-S.

Fire-types: Without Well-Baked Body, Fire-types like Iron Moth, Moltres, Cinderace, and Heatran easily defeat Corviknight.

Strong Special Attackers: Choice Specs Gholdengo and Primarina can 2HKO Corviknight even if it's running maximum HP and Special Defense EVs.

Physically Defensive.description:
Corviknight's great defensive typing and solid utility moves make it a defensive staple in the tier, checking physical threats like Roaring Moon, Zamazenta, and Great Tusk. Fluffy is the generally preferred ability, as it provides Corviknight with more physical bulk, letting it have a much easier time checking the aforementioned physical attackers, along with Kingambit. Intimidate gives Corviknight a better matchup into Chien-Pao, Mold Breaker Great Tusk, and the Ogerpon formes excluding Ogerpon-H. Corviknight favors Brave Bird due to its higher power and ability to deal significant damage to Great Tusk and Zamazenta, but Body Press can be used to hit Ting-Lu, Iron Treads, and Kingambit. U-turn helps Corviknight's allies by generating momentum, Roost gives it longevity, and Defog removes entry hazards to aid Stealth Rock-weak partners like Chien-Pao and Ogerpon-H. Rocky Helmet punishes Great Tusk and non-Magic Guard Roaring Moon and Zamazenta as well as the many U-turn pivots like Corviknight in the tier, while Leftovers provides Corviknight with extra longevity. Corviknight runs 0 Speed IVs and a Relaxed nature to grants its allies safe entry with U-turn.

Corviknight is used mostly on offense and balance structures that appreciate its defensive prowess, entry hazard removal, and ability to provide slow pivoting. RegenVest Pokemon like Swampert, Manaphy, and Primarina are heavily reliant on their Assault Vest, so Corviknight aids them by functioning as a Knock Off absorber from users like Roaring Moon, Great Tusk, and Iron Treads. Special attackers like Gholdengo, Zapdos, and Iron Moth are quite threatening to Corviknight, so specially defensive checks like Manaphy, Swampert, and Roaring Moon are much appreciated. Fire-types like Cinderace and Ogerpon-H scare Corviknight; therefore, it requires checks to them like Pecharunt, Moltres, and Primordial Sea Zapdos. If using Fluffy, Corviknight struggles against Chien-Pao and Ogerpon-W, so checks to them like Manaphy and Moltres, respectively, are quite helpful. If using Intimidate, Corviknight benefits from Pecharunt and Scream Tail to take on strong physical attackers like Choice Band Zamazenta.

Dual Screens.description:
With its remarkable defensive profile and utility options, Prankster Corviknight becomes an effective lead at setting dual screens to support its teammates. Prankster-boosted Taunt allows Corviknight to shut down foes like Great Tusk, Skarmory, and Regieleki from setting their entry hazards or own dual screens; stop Cinderace and opposing Corviknight from removing its dual screens with Court Change and Defog; prevent offensive threats like Zamazenta, all Ogerpon formes, and Iron Boulder from using it as setup fodder; block Trick from Choice item users like Scream Tail and Gholdengo from crippling it; and additionally stop defensive Pokemon like Gholdengo, Scream Tail, and non-Prankster Pecharunt from healing freely, letting its teammates overwhelm them more easily. U-turn makes use of Corviknight's middling Speed tier to support its teammates by bringing them in safely after stomaching an attack, and it's especially valuable against Dark-type foes like Chien-Pao, Kingambit, and Taunt Roaring Moon that can otherwise set up in front of or even shut down Corviknight.

Corviknight is exclusively found on dual screens hyper offense teams. As a result, high caliber offensive threats like Tail Glow Manaphy, IronPress Zamazenta, and Dragon Dance Roaring Moon pair well with it, as they appreciate Corviknight bringing them in safely through U-turn and its dual screens to set up more easily. However, Double Dance sweepers such as Iron Crown and Galarian Moltres benefit from said dual screens even more, as they require multiple turns of setup before wreaking havoc. Other setup sweepers like Iron Boulder, Iron Hands, and Kingambit have the added benefit of threatening Cinderace and Defoggers like opposing Corviknight and Mandibuzz—especially since Mandibuzz can use Defog freely even in the face of Corviknight—preventing them any free opportunities of ever removing Corviknight's dual screens. Since other foes like Zapdos, Iron Moth, and Dark-types like Roaring Moon and Kingambit can also use Corviknight as entry points, teammates that can come in afterwards to either threaten or use them as setup fodder are also beneficial, with the aforementioned Iron Boulder, Deoxys-S, Zamazenta, and Great Tusk capable of doing so. Furthermore, Corviknight's ability to prevent Stealth Rock from going up with Taunt also gives setup sweepers like Ogerpon-Hearthflame and Chien-Pao more opportunities to get rolling.

Immunity.description:
Corviknight's great utility and defensive typing, further bolstered by an immunity-granting ability, make it an excellent special wall and defensive pivot. Volt Absorb lets it counter Electric-types such as Zapdos and Sandy Shocks and helps against special attackers that rely on Electric-type coverage, such as Deoxys-S, Latios, and Iron Crown. Similarly, Well-Baked Body shuts down Ogerpon-H, Moltres, and non-Discharge Iron Moth. Brave Bird is the go-to STAB move, while Body Press is an option on Well-Baked Body sets to take advantage of the Defense boosts. U-turn generates momentum for Corviknight’s teammates, and Defog provides valuable hazard control. A Sassy nature with 0 Speed IVs is crucial for Corviknight's role as a slow pivot.

Corviknight fits on balance and bulky offense teams, where its longevity and consistency gives it a niche over Iron Treads. Corviknight can pivot in more offensive teammates like Chien-Pao and Ogerpon-H, which also appreciate its ability to remove entry hazards. Corviknight is threatened by physical wallbreakers such as Zamazenta and Ogerpon-W, making physically defensive walls like Great Tusk and Pecharunt good teammates. Corviknight can also be overwhelmed by neutral special attacks from Gholdengo and Primarina, so it needs backup from RegenVest users like Manaphy and Swampert. In exchange, Volt Absorb Corviknight helps Manaphy answer Electric-types, while Well-Baked Body helps Swampert answer Fire-types carrying Grass-type coverage like Iron Moth and Heatran.

Choice Band.description: With great offensive stats and a spammable move in Flip Turn, Barraskewda serves as an excellent offensive pivot that's capable of outpacing the majority of the metagame. Close Combat lets it deal with foes that resist its Water-type moves such as Roaring Moon, Ogerpon-W, and Empoleon, while Aqua Jet lets it pick off weakened foes that normally outspeed it such as Choice Scarf Sandy Shocks and Hisuian Electrode, as well as foes that have set up, such as Volcarona and Great Tusk. Drill Run and Psychic Fangs also threaten Water-resistant foes, with Drill Run OHKOing Desolate Land Fire-types such as Iron Moth, Heatran, and Ceruledge and Psychic Fangs threatening Quaquaval and Kommo-o. Barraskewda takes great advantage of weakened walls such as Corviknight and Deoxys-D, making physical wallbreakers such as Iron Boulder, Galarian Zapdos, and Ceruledge good partners. Specially offensive pivots such as Hisuian Electrode and Azelf form great VoltTurn cores with Barraskewda, since they're able to threaten each other's checks, with Barraskewda forcing out special walls such as Meloetta and Swampert while they threaten physical walls such as Corviknight and Quaquaval in return. Barraskewda is very frail and easily OHKOed by faster foes such as Choice Scarf Roaring Moon and Azelf, which makes defensive pivots such as Corviknight, Mandibuzz, and Swampert necessary. It also struggles a lot with entry hazards, making hazard removers such as Great Tusk and Corviknight appreciated.

Zamazenta.comments:
Other Options

Howl is an excellent move on all-out attacking variants that trade a coverage option for the ability to set up and punish predicted switches harder. Fire Fang is an option on Choice Band and all-out attacking variants that allows Zamazenta to hit Fluffy Corviknight and Skarmory, with the former variant threatening to 2HKO Fluffy Corviknight with light chip damage. Wild Charge, meanwhile, hits Primarina hard while being a solid midground option due to its non-negligible damage output. Heavy Slam is an option on IronPress variants to deal hefty damage to Scream Tail, which would wall Zamazenta and force it out otherwise, while Substitute allows Zamazenta to set up without fear of status and making it harder to revenge kill. Magic Guard + Life Orb variants can consider using Moonblast to lure in and remove Fluffy Great Tusk, which can otherwise check Zamazenta consistently, but this is niche at best and not worth using over other moves.

Checks and Counters

Physical Walls: Physical walls such as Fluffy Corviknight, Skarmory, and Great Tusk can consistently switch into Zamazenta and force it out or take the opportunity to set up or remove entry hazards.

Physically Defensive Ghost-types: Pecharunt is a good counter to Zamazenta, being capable of consistently surviving multiple uses of Crunch and threatening it in return with Malignant Chain, while Gholdengo and Sinistcha, when equipped with Fluffy, can threaten Zamazenta out or use it as setup fodder, respectively.

Fairy-types: Scream Tail and Primarina are capable of surviving most of Zamazenta's attacks and force it out in return.

Fast Psychic-types: Deoxys-S, Choice Scarf Latios, and Choice Scarf Azelf all outspeed and threaten to OHKO Zamazenta or force it out.

Choice Band.description:
Choice Band Zamazenta leverages its amazing Speed tier, good Attack, great natural bulk, and spammable STAB Close Combat to serve as one of the tier's most consistent and dangerous wallbreakers and revenge killers. Crunch smacks Ghost- and Psychic-types like Pecharunt, Gholdengo, and Latios hard; Stone Edge OHKOes Flying-types like Zapdos and Moltres while being Zamazenta's strongest move against Fluffy Corviknight and Skarmory; and Heavy Slam threatens a would-be check in Scream Tail while dealing hefty damage to Primarina.

Zamazenta fits on numerous playstyles that are in need of a fast and deadly wallbreaker that also acts as a stopgap to Dark-types like Roaring Moon, Kingambit, and Chien-Pao. This makes teammates that are scared of them, like Latios, Gholdengo, and Pecharunt, good partners, with the former two helping to alleviate Zamazenta's inability to break past physical walls like Pecharunt, Fluffy Corviknight, and Great Tusk. Furthermore, Zamazenta helps to threaten the special walls Latios and Gholdengo may struggle with, such as RegenVest Swampert, Manaphy, and Iron Treads. Zamazenta appreciates entry hazard support from setters like Garchomp, Scream Tail, and Ting-Lu, allowing it to wear down physical walls faster and overwhelm them.

All-out Attacker.description:
Zamazenta functions as an excellent revenge killer and late-game cleaner, leveraging its amazing Speed to outspeed and force out metagame staples such as Roaring Moon, Kingambit, and Chien-Pao. Crunch hits Ghost-types like Pecharunt and Gholdengo that want to switch into Close Combat. Stone Edge hits Flying-types such as Zapdos and Moltres, while Heavy Slam threatens Primarina and Scream Tail. Magic Guard lets Zamazenta ignore damage from entry hazards, Rocky Helmet, and Life Orb, while Sword of Ruin in conjunction with Expert Belt helps it pick up key KOs, notably guaranteeing OHKOs on Zapdos and Scream Tail.

Zamazenta fits on more offensive teams where it can afford to trade the power from Choice Band for the ability to switch moves, letting it clean up weakened teams. Magic Guard variants also fit well on entry hazard-stacking teams with partners like Skarmory and Ting-Lu since it appreciates the additional chip damage on walls like Pecharunt and Great Tusk while ignoring opposing hazards itself. Zamazenta needs other physical wallbreakers such as Ogerpon-H and Kingambit to help it break common physical walls like Corviknight and Pecharunt; in exchange, Zamazenta provides speed control, checks Roaring Moon, and can overwhelm Great Tusk. Alternatively, special attackers like Zapdos and Latios also threaten the aforementioned physical walls while pivoting Zamazenta in on special walls such as Iron Treads and Swampert. Zamazenta can be forced out by Deoxys-S and Choice Scarf Azelf, so it appreciates RegenVest pivots such as Manaphy and Swampert. Zamazenta's high defensive stats let it serve as an emergency check to Chien-Pao, the Ogerpon formes, and the aforementioned Roaring Moon, outspeeding and threatening them.

IronPress.description:
IronPress Zamazenta serves as a deadly wincon that provides great defensive utility, leveraging its excellent bulk and useful Dark resistance to switch into dangerous threats like Chien-Pao, Kingambit, and Roaring Moon and use these opportunities to set up or wallbreak. Stone Edge threatens Flying-types like Zapdos and Moltres and is Zamazenta's strongest move against Fluffy Landorus-T, while Crunch hits Ghost-types like Pecharunt, Gholdengo, and Sinistcha. The given Attack EVs allow Stone Edge to OHKO Moltres without Expert Belt. With Expert Belt, Crunch 2HKOes physically defensive Gholdengo after minimal chip damage and has near-guaranteed odds of 3HKOing non-Intimidate Pecharunt; this item also allows Stone Edge to OHKO offensive Zapdos. The remaining EVs are invested into Defense to power up Body Press and let Zamazenta answer physical threats better.

IronPress Zamazenta best fits on both balance and offensive teams that appreciate its defensive utility and potency as a wincon. Offensive Pokemon such as Roaring Moon, Zapdos, and Gholdengo that can lure in setup fodder like Great Tusk, Roaring Moon, Kingambit, and Swampert for Zamazenta pair well with it. Bulky Fighting-resistant foes unafraid of Zamazenta's coverage like Pecharunt, Primarina, and defensive Landorus-I are its biggest roadblocks; thus, teammates that can deal with them such as Roaring Moon, Kingambit, and Latios pair well with it. Kingambit is an especially notable partner, as it complements Zamazenta both offensively and defensively extremely well, with Kingambit switching into and threatening the Ghost- and Psychic-types Zamazenta detests, while Zamazenta deals with Kingambit's checks like Great Tusk, Iron Hands, and Body Press Corviknight. Zamazenta is forced out by threats that can either outspeed and threaten it or survive a hit and attack in return like Latios, Deoxys-S, and Scream Tail; thus, teammates that can deal with them like Iron Treads, Swampert, and Manaphy are greatly appreciated.

Zamazenta-C leverages its Fighting / Steel typing and high Defense and Speed to be a decent Body Press sweeper that's able to switch in and set up on common Pokemon like Corviknight, Chien-Pao, and Roaring Moon. Stone Edge helps against Zapdos, Iron Moth, and Ceruledge, while Substitute helps it set up against would-be switch-ins like Iron Hands and Great Tusk as well as shielding it from status afflictions from moves like Manaphy's Scald and Corrosion Pecharun't Toxic. Zamazenta-C plays best on offensive teams, so it appreciates entry hazards from Pokemon like Skarmory and Deoxys-D and dual screens support from Pokemon like Corviknight and Deoxys-S, which help it take KOs and set up, respectively. Zamazenta-C also appreciates Pokemon like Chien-Pao, Roaring Moon, and Gengar that are able to take down Deoxys-D, Gholdengo, and Pecharunt, as otherwise it is unable to make progress at all.

Dual Screens.description: With the highest base Speed in the game, Regieleki can enable high-powered setup sweepers like Manaphy, Kingambit, and Iron Hands on hyper offense teams as a dual screens lead, though it's more niche than other dual screens setters. With Refrigerate, Rapid Spin is a guaranteed way to remove entry hazards even in the face of Ghost-types like Gholdengo and Pecharunt. However, Regieleki isn't the best to rely on for Rapid Spin all of the time, so teammates like Great Tusk and Quaquaval can help as additional spinners. Explosion lets Regieleki get its teammates in safely, do massive damage to foes like Swampert, Great Tusk, and Roaring Moon, and prevent dual screens removal via Defog. Many setup sweepers, most notably Gholdengo and Iron Hands, appreciate Regieleki's dual screens support while also fending off priority from Pokemon like Slither Wing and Prankster Corviknight, which would otherwise prevent it from setting dual screens. Regieleki similarly doesn't enjoy Choice Scarf Roaring Moon and Meowscarada, as they are the only relevant Pokemon able to outspeed it instantly, so having checks to them such as Kingambit, Iron Hands, and Zamazenta can help. As a final note, Regieleki is exclusively a hyper offense lead meant to support Pokemon such as Iron Boulder, Gengar, and Kingambit by providing them with extra opportunities to set up and win the game.

Ursaluna-Bloodmoon.comments:
Other Options

Ursaluna-B can run a number of other immunity abilities like Storm Drain, Well-Baked Body, Bulletproof, and even Sap Sipper if the team needs the immunity, but they all have a much narrower range of setup opportunities compared to Fluffy. To beat Ground-immune Ghost-types, Ursaluna-B can either run Snarl at the expense of Calm Mind or Moonlight or run Mold Breaker or Mind's Eye to still hit them with its main moves. Substitute lets it punish slow pivots without having to predict, but it is generally less useful than its main moves. Offensive wallbreaking sets like Tinted Lens and Adaptability can be run on more offensive teams to provide more immediate power, but they mostly beat the same threats as Fluffy, just more quickly. Lastly, Steam Engine Weakness Policy Ursaluna-B is very funny on dual screens hyper offense, as it outspeeds everything at +6 and OHKOes almost everything at +2, but it is very unreliable.

Checks and Counters

Earth Eater Ghost-types: Most Ursaluna-B sets are utterly unable to damage Ground-immune Ghost-types, notably Gholdengo and Pecharunt.

Non-contact Physical Attackers: Ogerpon formes, Chien-Pao, and Cinderace can all OHKO Ursaluna-B, as they bypass Fluffy.

Special Wallbreakers: While they can be hard to switch in safely, special wallbreakers like Latios, Iron Moth, and Gholdengo have no trouble forcing Ursaluna-B off the field, often even after a Calm Mind.

Bulky Calm Mind.description:
Ursaluna-B is an unusual combination of wallbreaker and physical wall, leveraging its outstanding bulk with Fluffy to check important threats like Roaring Moon and Kingambit, set up with Calm Mind, and break open defensive cores that usually cannot slow pivot against it with boosted Blood Moon. With Fluffy, it can even tank hits from Zamazenta and Great Tusk and OHKO back with Blood Moon. Water Absorb is an alternative that lets it block Flip Turn from Swampert and Primarina, turn Manaphy into setup fodder, and check Choice Specs Primordial Sea users, but makes it much less reliable of a physical check. Blood Moon and Earth Power have excellent neutral coverage, letting it run both Calm Mind and Moonlight to set up comfortably on more passive teams. With 13 Speed IVs, it underspeeds level 99 Corviknight; this turns what would be a reliable pivot into setup fodder, as Corviknight cannot Roost or pivot safely without the switch-in risking a Blood Moon.

Ursaluna-B fits well on balance and offensive teams, as it provides a notable defensive presence while still putting pressure on or holes in opposing teams. Faster special attackers like Deoxys-S, Zapdos, and Gholdengo appreciate Ursaluna-B wearing down and potentially breaking through common shared checks like RegenVest Manaphy. Even physical attackers benefit from it, as Corviknight and Great Tusk will commonly come in on it to recover or attempt to revenge kill it, only to find themselves chipped, KOed, or set up on. Ursaluna-B's special bulk is very poor, even with a Calm Mind up, so solid specially defensive teammates like its own RegenVest Manaphy are highly appreciated. Due to Fluffy's drawbacks, Ursaluna-B is unable to check some common physical attackers, especially the Ogerpon formes, Chien-Pao, and Pyro Ball Cinderace; at least one other reliable secondary physically defensive Pokemon like Pecharunt, Corviknight, or Regenerator Primarina is near-mandatory. Ursaluna-B is unable to hit Earth Eater / Levitate Ghost-types like Pecharunt and Gholdengo, so a Dark-type like Roaring Moon or Ting-Lu is also recommended.

Meowscarada.comments:
Other Options

Heavy-Duty Boots and Magic Guard + Life Orb sets are niche options that can fit on bulkier teams that appreciate the longevity of such sets given Meowscarada's propensity to pivot often, though the loss in power is often noticeable such as being unable to 2HKO Pecharunt. Mixed sets with Adaptability + Life Orb and Dark Pulse are a niche option to bait checks such as Corviknight. Spikes and Toxic Spikes can also be considered as options to accrue chip damage or force entry hazard removal by Corviknight. Sucker Punch can be considered on Choice Band sets if a team needs priority to check faster threats such as Deoxys-S and Choice Scarf Gholdengo. Lash Out is a niche option that allows Meowscarada to better threaten Intimidate Corviknight and Skarmory.

Checks and Counters

Corviknight and Skarmory: With their significant bulk and 4x resistance to Grass, Meowscarada can struggle greatly in breaking through these counters alone, particularly Fluffy variants, and so requires support from teammates to weaken or remove these foes.

Faster Threats: Meowscarada's lack of bulk and poor defensive typing means Choice Band sets are vulnerable to most offensive threats that outspeed it such as Chien-Pao, Zamazenta, and Deoxys-S as well as various Choice Scarf users such as Roaring Moon and Azelf. Choice Scarf sets are also vulnerable to priority from the likes of Chien-Pao and Kingambit.

Choice Item.description:
With its good Attack and great Speed tier, Meowscarada serves as a strong offensive pivot and wallbreaker that is able to exploit and outspeed common defensive and offensive staples such as Great Tusk, Roaring Moon, and Gholdengo. Choice Band enables Meowscarada to secure OHKOs against foes such as Scream Tail, Pecharunt, and Great Tusk as well as pressure checks such as Corviknight. Choice Scarf guarantees Meowscarada is able to outspeed the entire unboosted metagame and threaten other Choice Scarf users such as Roaring Moon, Latios, and Scream Tail. U-turn lets Meowscarada scout switch-ins and accumulate chip damage against checks such as Corviknight. Triple Axel threatens checks such as Mandibuzz and defensive Zapdos while also OHKOing targets like Landorus and Garchomp. Low Kick alternatively allows Meowscarada to hit bulky Steel-types such as Cobalion, Heatran, and Kingambit. Both Triple Axel and Low Kick OHKO Roaring Moon. Trick cripples checks such as Corviknight or setup sweepers such as Fluffy Roaring Moon, Iron Crown, and Kingambit.

Meowscarada can fit on a variety of balanced and more offensive teams that appreciate it as a wallbreaker and value its Speed tier above other dangerous offensive threats. Meowscarada can struggle to get onto the field due to its lack of bulk and so appreciates slow pivots like Corviknight, Swampert, and Pecharunt that can also absorb revenge killing attempts from the likes of Chien-Pao, Deoxys-S, and Zamazenta. Meowscarada can also benefit from the presence of offensive pivots like Cinderace, Moltres, and Azelf that provide it with opportunities against switch-ins like Great Tusk, Manaphy, and Swampert. Meowscarada can struggle with breaking through Corviknight and Skarmory and so pairs well with other strong wallbreakers such as Zamazenta, Chien-Pao, and Ogerpon-W that can pressure and overload them. Similarly, it can benefit from strong hazard setters such as Great Tusk, Landorus, Ting-Lu, and Heatran that can chip and weaken Corviknight and other checks like Iron Hands and Zamazenta. Alternatively, Meowscarada can be paired with other strong wallbreakers such as Choice Specs Gholdengo and that can take advantage of a free switch into Corviknight or Skarmory.

The Salt Cure.description: Garganacl is a tough defensive wall that uses Salt Cure to wear down defensive Pokemon, like Swampert, Great Tusk, and Corviknight, and utilizes its ability selection to check a variety of different Pokemon. Fluffy allows Garganacl to check Kingambit and Iron Boulder, Water Absorb lets it reliably switch in on defensive Manaphy as well as help with checking Choice-locked Water-types like Barraskewda and Primarina, and Earth Eater lets it check Roaring Moon and Dragonite running Earthquake. Stealth Rock lets Garganacl support its teammates by providing entry hazards, while with Iron Defense or Curse, Garganacl can set up on and more easily check Pokemon like Iron Hands, Ceruledge, and Dragonite, and boost Body Press or Earthquake, depending on the set. When using Body Press, Garganacl can more efficiently take on Chien-Pao as well as a majority of other Pokemon, while with Earthquake, it can deal with Iron Boulder and Pecharunt. Protect lets Garganacl deal extra damage with Salt Cure active as well as gain some extra Leftovers recovery. Garganacl can either use Leftovers or Heavy-Duty Boots, the former letting it gain more recovery through Protect and the latter helping Garganacl switch in more often. Garganacl provides great defensive support to frail offensive Pokemon, like Iron Moth, Gengar, and Galarian Zapdos, as a to check faster Pokemon like Roaring Moon, Dragonite, and Iron Boulder. Garganacl can struggle against special attackers, such as Deoxys-S, Azelf, and offensive Gholdengo, so having specially defensive teammates, like Hisuian Goodra, Manaphy, and Blissey, can help. Garganacl enjoys being on more defensive teams for having teammates that can take on physical attackers with it, such as Pecharunt, Mandibuzz, and Primarina, as it struggles to reliably check Pokemon like Chien-Pao, Zamazenta, and Barraskewda.

Brambleghast.comments:
Other Options

Spikes can be used over Skitter Smack and Rapid Spin to take advantage of the switches that Brambleghast forces, helping to wear down the opposing team.

Checks and Counters

Bulky Dark- and Grass-resistant Pokemon: Bulky Pokemon that resist both of Brambleghast's STAB attacks such as Mandibuzz, Kingambit, and Roaring Moon are difficult to deal with, as they can switch in safely and threaten to OHKO Brambleghast due to its low bulk.

Faster Offensive Threats: Since Brambleghast is so frail, faster offensive Pokemon such as Cinderace, Latios, and Zamazenta can all OHKO it without fear.

Choice Band.description:
The combination of Choice Band and its powerful offensive typing makes Brambleghast a dangerous wallbreaker. Poltergeist threatens common defensive Pokemon such as Pecharunt, Corviknight, and Scream Tail, while Power Whip OHKOes non-Fluffy Great Tusk, Ting-Lu , and common Water-types such as Manaphy and Primarina. Skitter Smack is used to OHKO Roaring Moon variants , that can otherwise switch in, live one hit, (AC) and then force Brambleghast out. Rapid Spin allows Brambleghast to take advantage of the switches it forces to clear entry hazards. Rapid Spin is especially useful, (AC) as Ghost-types that could otherwise block Rapid Spin do not want to switch into Brambleghast. Shadow Sneak is used to revenge kill weakened, faster Pokemon such as Deoxys-S, Latios, Iron Crown, and Iron Moth. Sword of Ruin is the preferred ability because it allows Brambleghast to hit hard with both its STAB moves and coverage, while Adaptability can be used instead if a teammate is running Sword of Ruin. An Adamant nature is used to maximize its wallbreaking ability.

Due to Brambleghast's frailty, it often struggles to switch into even defensive Pokemon, making slow pivot move users such as U-turn Corviknight and RegenVest Primarina valuable teammates. Many special attackers such as Latios, Gholdengo, and Iron Crown pair well with Brambleghast, as it threatens most of the common specially defensive Pokemon in the tier. Physical attackers that rely on contact moves such as Slither Wing and Zamazenta also make good partners because Brambleghast's Poltergeist does not make contact, allowing it to beat walls such as Fluffy Corviknight and Great Tusk. Fast Pokemon such as Deoxys-S and Chien-Pao or priority users such as Kingambit can clean up teams after Brambleghast has weakened opposing walls.

Bulk Up.description: Palafin is an extremely niche setup sweeper that has one of the strongest priority moves in the tier and not much else, as it is forced to use Zero to Hero to transform. Substitute prevents it from being statused from Pecharunt, Gholdengo, and Manaphy while allowing it to set up on passive targets like Corviknight, Swampert, and the aforementioned Manaphy. Bulk Up makes up for its lack of power, since it lacks an ability and item that increases its damage output. Drain Punch keeps Palafin healthy against checks like Corviknight and Manaphy and allows it to damage Water-immune foes like Iron Moth and Heatran. Ice Punch smacks Dragon-types like Dragonite and Roaring Moon, while Zen Headbutt OHKOes Iron Moth. Palafin fits on bulkier or balance teams as a late-game sweeper, so partners that can wear the opposing team down over time like Magic Guard Roaring Moon, Hisuian Electrode, and Deoxys-S are appreciated; entry hazard setters like Scream Tail, Heatran, and Great Tusk are also beneficial with their ability to chip down foes over time. Palafin is checked offensively by threats like Dragonite, Desolate Land Ceruledge, and Scream Tail, so teammates like Corviknight, Well-Baked Body Mandibuzz, and Heatran handle these threats. Physical walls like Pecharunt and Skarmory can wall Palafin consistently, so partners that can force them out like Deoxys-S and Sandy Shocks pair well with it. Desolate Land users like Iron Moth Roaring Moon, Primordial Sea Zapdos, and Well-Baked Body Gholdengo can deal with Desolate Land users like Iron Moth, Cinderace, and Heatran, making for great partners.

Great Tusk.comments:
Other Options

Wandering Spirit is a niche ability on physically defensive sets that prevents Regenerator Pokemon like Swampert and Roaring Moon from regaining their HP when they pivot out while also letting Great Tusk gain Regenerator. Magic Guard + Life Orb is a great combination for offensive sets, as this prevents Great Tusk from being chipped down by Rocky Helmet and poison from Malignant Chain or Toxic Spikes; it also lets Great Tusk make use of Supercell Slam to hit Corviknight and Skarmory as well as Head Smash for Zapdos and Moltres without taking any recoil. Mold Breaker is used on offensive sets to allow Great Tusk to bypass Fluffy used on Pokemon like Corviknight, Skarmory, and Great Tusk as well as Levitate and Earth Eater on Gholdengo and Pecharunt. Earth Eater gives Great Tusk immunity to Earthquake from Ting-Lu, Swampert, and opposing Great Tusk while also letting it counter Sandy Shocks. Great Tusk can opt for an EV spread of 252 HP / 4 Atk / 252 Spe to make it a fast utility Pokemon while still retaining its defensive prowess. Eject Button is a niche item that swings the momentum in your favor against Pokemon trying to pivot out with U-turn such as Roaring Moon and Corviknight.

Checks and Counters

Special Attackers: Great Tusk's incredibly low Special Defense makes it prone to being OHKOed by special attackers like Deoxys-S, Zapdos, Scream Tail, Gholdengo, and Iron Moth.

Physical Attackers With Super Effective Moves: Chien-Pao, Iron Boulder, Meowscarada, and the Ogerpon formes can all 2HKO Great Tusk with their super effective attacks.

Residual Damage: Residual damage like poison, Spikes, and Rocky Helmet all slowly chip away at Great Tusk, which is compounded by it lacking any sort of longevity if it isn't using Regenerator or Leftovers.

Physically Defensive.description:
Thanks to its enormous physical bulk, solid utility options, and wide variety of abilities it can use, Great Tusk functions as an excellent physical wall. Earthquake lets Great Tusk hit foes that would otherwise beat it like Pecharunt, Scream Tail, and defensive Gholdengo, whereas Body Press does more damage than Earthquake due to its defensive investment and does solid damage to non-Fluffy Corviknight and Skarmory. Knock Off allows Great Tusk to annoy switch-ins like Corviknight, Skarmory, and opposing Great Tusk by removing their items, while Ice Spinner does good damage to Zapdos. Rapid Spin allows Great Tusk to support its teammates by removing entry hazards. Stealth Rock lets Great Tusk residually chip Pokemon like Chien-Pao, Deoxys-S, and Heatran, while Taunt prevents Corviknight from using Defog, Skarmory from setting up hazards, and both of them from recovering with Roost. Scrappy lets Great Tusk use Rapid Spin against Ghost-types such as Gholdengo and Pecharunt while also being able to hit Gholdengo with Body Press; however, Regenerator gives Great Tusk longevity, which lets it check Roaring Moon and opposing Great Tusk without being scared of getting chipped down, but this means that Great Tusk is forced to be used with non-RegenVest special walls. Alternatively, Fluffy makes Great Tusk incredibly bulky, letting it easily switch in on Choice Band Zamazenta and Roaring Moon. Leftovers is appreciated on Scrappy and Fluffy sets, as Great Tusk lacks longevity, but Rocky Helmet is also an option to punish Corviknight, opposing Great Tusk, Zamazenta, and non-Magic Guard Roaring Moon. Heavy-Duty Boots is a bit more niche, but it lets Great Tusk remove hazards without being affected by them.

Defensive Great Tusk is most often used on bulky offense or balance teams. Attackers that are easily chipped down by entry hazards like Chien-Pao, Deoxys-S, and non-Magic Guard Roaring Moon appreciate Great Tusk removing them with Rapid Spin. Teammates like Zapdos, Roaring Moon, and Iron Moth enjoy the Stealth Rock that Great Tusk sets up, as they can pivot out to slowly chip away at switch-ins such as Corviknight and RegenVest Roaring Moon. Great Tusk's low Special Defense requires special walls like RegenVest Manaphy, Swampert, and Roaring Moon as well as Heatran, Iron Treads, and Empoleon for Regenerator variants of Great Tusk. Great Tusk is also vulnerable to physical attackers like Chien-Pao, Iron Boulder, Meowscarada, and the Ogerpon formes; therefore, it appreciates aid from Pokemon such as physically defensive Manaphy, Skarmory, and Moltres.

Offensive.description:
Offensive Great Tusk is an excellent spinner and wallbreaker due to its ability to threaten common spinblockers like Pecharunt and Gholdengo while serving as a great stopgap to the numerous Dark-types the tier has to offer in Kingambit and Roaring Moon. Knock Off is an extremely spammable move that cripples Great Tusk's switch-ins like Zapdos and Moltres, while Head Smash is an excellent option on Magic Guard sets for its ability to let Great Tusk lure in and remove the aforementioned Flying-types. Rapid Spin allows Great Tusk to provide more utility to its team and potentially snowball out of control when also using Bulk Up. Smack Down allows Great Tusk to deal hefty damage to Zapdos and Moltres as well as ground Flying-types like Corviknight, Skarmory, and the Landorus formes, letting it follow up with Headlong Rush. Taunt denies Roost attempts from Great Tusk's checks like Corviknight and Skarmory, Defog from the former, and setup sweepers like IronPress Zamazenta and Swords Dance Kingambit from setting up. Leftovers provides Great Tusk with much-needed longevity, Earth Plate allows Headlong Rush to 2HKO physically defensive Manaphy after mild chip damage, and Life Orb should exclusively be used with Magic Guard to gain a boost on all of Great Tusk's moves that has no drawbacks. Mold Breaker allows Great Tusk to bypass abilities that would hinder it from its checks like Fluffy Corviknight and Skarmory and Levitate or Earth Eater Gholdengo and Iron Hands. Scrappy allows Great Tusk to guarantee a Rapid Spin even in the face of would-be spinblockers while giving it an immunity to Intimidate, making Intimidate Corviknight and Skarmory much shakier checks to it. Magic Guard allows it to ignore passive chip damage and thus boosts its longevity, allowing it to switch in more consistently throughout a game without fear.

Offensive Great Tusk fits best on offensive and bulky offense teams that appreciate its ability to stave off common anti-offense threats like Swords Dance Kingambit and Choice Scarf Roaring Moon while being able to consistently remove entry hazards. Great Tusk dreads special attackers and thus requires good specially defensive partners like RegenVest Roaring Moon, Manaphy, and Swampert. Great Tusk is unable to check many physical threats that can hit it super effectively like the Ogerpon formes and Chien-Pao, making secondary physical walls like Intimidate Pecharunt and the aforementioned Manaphy good teammates. Other physical wallbreakers that appreciate Great Tusk's ability to break through Pecharunt and Fluffy Corviknight and Skarmory, like Zamazenta, Roaring Moon, and Kingambit, pair well with it.

Choice Band.description:
Great Tusk's powerful STAB moves, massive attack stat, and Choice Band make it one of the strongest wallbreakers in the tier. Knock Off cripples Moltres and Zapdos by removing their Heavy-Duty Boots while helping to make progress against Corviknight and opposing Great Tusk by removing Rocky Helmet or Leftovers. It is also required on Sword of Ruin sets to hit Earth Eater Ghost-types such as Gholdengo and Pecharunt. Ice Spinner handles Flying-types like Zapdos and the Landorus formes that Great Tusk would otherwise struggle with, and Rapid Spin provides useful entry hazard control. Mold Breaker lets Great Tusk completely ignore Earth Eater on the aforementioned Gholdengo and Pecharunt, as well as bypassing Fluffy on Corviknight, Skarmory, and opposing Great Tusk. Sword of Ruin trades these matchups for more power, notably letting Great Tusk potentially 2HKO Corviknight through Intimidate.

Choice Band Great Tusk works best on offense and balance teams, where other physical attackers can make up for its subpar Speed and take advantage of weakened physically defensive walls. Great Tusk can tailor its ability to the team's need, such as running Sword of Ruin when paired with Chien-Pao, thus being able to break past Corviknight regardless of whether it has Intimidate or Fluffy. Similarly, Choice Band Zamazenta can handle Intimidate Corviknight, while Mold Breaker Great Tusk helps threaten both Fluffy Corviknight and Pecharunt. Due to its low Speed and Special Defense, Great Tusk is vulnerable to special attackers such as Latios, Deoxys, and Scream Tail; special walls like Manaphy, Iron Treads, and Swampert can check these threats while also pivoting Great Tusk in.

Scream Tail.comments:
Other Options

Calm Mind lets Scream Tail become a threatening setup sweeper, letting it 2HKO RegenVest Swampert and Manaphy at +2 while also allowing it to potentially outlast switch-ins like Iron Moth and Moltres. Unaware + Calm Mind is a solid strategy on hyper offense teams to let Scream Tail set up and obliterate the foe with Stored Power and destroy Roaring Moon and Chien-Pao with Dazzling Gleam. Fire Blast or Flamethrower allow Scream Tail to break through Steel-types like Corviknight and Iron Treads that it usually struggles against. Choice Specs lets Scream Tail 3HKO RegenVest Manaphy and Swampert while 2HKOing them without their Assault Vest, but Scream Tail usually prefers not to use a Choice item due to its wide variety of utility moves.

Checks and Counters

Steel-types: Steel-types like Iron Treads, Heatran, and Gholdengo resist both Boomburst and Psychic Noise and can threaten Scream Tail with their STAB moves.

Specially Defensive Pokemon: RegenVest Swampert and Manaphy can switch into Scream Tail with ease and easily pivot out to an offensive threat.

Fast Offensive Threats: Without a Choice Scarf, Scream Tail gets outsped and OHKOed by Zamazenta, Choice Scarf Gholdengo, and Choice Band Roaring Moon.

Pixilate.description:
Thanks to its fantastic utility movepool, solid defensive stats and typing, and access to Boomburst + Pixilate, Scream Tail functions as a strong attacker, Stealth Rock setter, and decent defensive switch-in to threats like Iron Hands, Latios, and Deoxys-S. Boomburst + Pixilate enables Scream Tail to deal considerable damage, 2HKOing common threats like Zapdos, Kingambit, and RegenVest Iron Hands while bypassing Substitute from Zamazenta and OHKOing it. Psychic Noise hits Iron Moth and Pecharunt on top of preventing switch-ins like Corviknight and Gholdengo from healing; however, Stealth Rock is also an option. Wish grants Scream Tail longevity as well as letting it heal partners low on HP. Protect lets it guarantee healing from Wish, heal more from Leftovers, and scout the move that dangerous Choice item attackers like Gholdengo, Roaring Moon, and Zamazenta are using. Trick should be used exclusively on Choice Scarf sets to punish switch-ins like RegenVest Swampert and Manaphy as well as Iron Treads and Corviknight, benefiting other special wallbreakers like Gholdengo and Latios. Leftovers provides Scream Tail with extra longevity, Heavy-Duty Boots prevents chip damage from entry hazards, and Choice Scarf gives it valuable speed control, letting it outspeed non-Choice Scarf Roaring Moon, Zamazenta, and Deoxys-S.

Scream Tail is best suited for bulky offense and balance teams that make use of its offensive and defensive utility as well as its ability to set Stealth Rock and threaten or sit on most entry hazard removers with ease. Teammates that lack longevity like non-Regenerator Great Tusk, the Landorus formes, and Iron Treads appreciate Scream Tail for its ability to provide them healing with Wish. Teammates like Gholdengo and Iron moth that struggle with removing RegenVest Iron Hands and Roaring Moon benefit from Scream Tail being able to easily beat them with Boomburst; in return, Gholdengo and Iron Moth can threaten opposing Gholdengo, Iron Treads, and Assault Vest Iron Crown. Knock Off users like Roaring Moon, Great Tusk, and Manaphy can help Scream Tail by removing Assault Vest from Swampert and opposing Manaphy, letting it 3HKO them. Scream Tail is threatened by entry hazards if it is not running Heavy-Duty Boots, so it requires an entry hazard remover like Corviknight, Great Tusk, or Iron Treads.

Iron Treads.comments:
Other Options

Earth Power can be used over Earthquake to deal more damage to Great Tusk and Pecharunt, but the higher power of Earthquake is generally more appreciated. An mixed attacking set of Magic Guard + Life Orb with Steel Beam and Earthquake can be used due to its potential to break through Great Tusk and Swampert relatively easily, but this is a very niche set that struggles to break through Corviknight and Manaphy. Immunity abilities can be used to cover for some of the weaknesses Iron Treads has: Earth Eater for Great Tusk, Ting-Lu, and the Landorus formes; Water Absorb for Manaphy and Primarina; and Well-Baked Body for Iron Moth and Heatran. Bulletproof sets can run Assault Vest if not running Stealth Rock for the extra bulk against strong special attackers like Zapdos, Deoxys-S, and Latios, but without Leftovers, Iron Treads lacks any form of recovery.

Checks and Counters

Physical Attackers: Physical attackers like Roaring Moon, Zamazenta, and Chien-Pao easily destroy Iron Treads with their STAB moves.

Ground-types: Great Tusk, Ting-Lu, and the Landorus formes block Volt Switch and can demolish Iron Treads with Earthquake.

Strong Special Attackers: Strong special attackers like Choice Specs Latios, Gholdengo, and Deoxys-S can break through Iron Treads's non-RegenVest set with their STAB moves due to Iron Treads's low special bulk and lack of recovery.

Specially Defensive.description:
Iron Treads's amazing defensive typing, access to incredible utility moves, and decent defensive stats let it function as a specially defensive staple for many teams. Rapid Spin lets it support Stealth Rock-weak teammates like Chien-Pao and Ogerpon-H by removing entry hazards. Knock Off punishes switch-ins like Corviknight and Great Tusk by removing their Leftovers or Rocky Helmet, whereas Ice Spinner punishes would-be switch-ins like Garchomp and the Landorus formes. Volt Switch gives Iron Treads the ability to pivot and provide its teammates with momentum, while Stealth Rock offers valuable role compression. Bulletproof grants it immunity to Shadow Ball from Gholdengo, Weather Ball from Moltres and Zapdos, Pyro Ball from Cinderace, and Focus Blast from Deoxys-S, making Iron Treads a great switch-in to those Pokemon. 12 Speed EVs and a Jolly nature let Iron Treads outspeed Zamazenta at +1 and maximum Speed Modest Gholdengo at +0.

Iron Treads is best suited for more offense-based teams due to its subpar special bulk and lack of reliable recovery, which it makes up for with much-needed role compression. Iron Treads excellently complements physically defensive RegenVest Manaphy, as Iron Treads can check Zapdos, Latios, and Gholdengo for Manaphy, while it checks Chien-Pao and Great Tusk for Iron Treads. Physical attackers like Zamazenta, Roaring Moon, and Great Tusk pose a problem for Iron Treads; therefore, physically defensive partners like Pecharunt and Skarmory are appreciated. Primordial Sea partners like Zapdos, Ogerpon-W, and Primarina are helpful to deal with Fire-types like Iron Moth, Heatran, and Ogerpon-H that can destroy Iron Treads with ease.

RegenVest.description:
Due to an amazing defensive typing, decent defensive stats, and an incredible utility movepool, Iron Treads functions as a niche RegenVest user capable of checking many special attackers like Gholdengo, Latios, and Scream Tail. Earthquake is generally preferred as Iron Treads's Ground-type STAB move due to it being the strongest option, but Earth Power deals more damage to Great Tusk and Pecharunt. Knock Off annoys defensive staples like Corviknight and Great Tusk by removing their Leftovers or Rocky Helmet while debilitating Manaphy and Swampert by getting rid of their Assault Vest; alternatively, Ice Spinner hits Zapdos, Garchomp, and the Landorus formes. Rapid Spin supports allies that dislike entry hazards like Chien-Pao and Ogerpon-H. Volt Switch provides Iron Treads with pivoting while chipping Corviknight and Skarmory lacking Volt Absorb.

Iron Treads fits best on more defensively oriented team structures such as bulky offense and stall. Physical attackers like Zamazenta, Cinderace, and Garchomp all threaten Iron Treads with their STAB moves, so checks to these Pokemon such as Pecharunt and Skarmory are an important asset. Iron Treads struggles against strong special attackers like Iron Moth, Primarina, and Choice Specs Gholdengo; therefore, partners like Roaring Moon and Moltres can aid it. Since Iron Treads is heavily reliant on Assault Vest to bolster its subpar special bulk, it needs Knock Off absorbers like Skarmory and Scream Tail. Ground-types like Great Tusk, Ting-Lu, and the Landorus formes are especially annoying to Iron Treads, as they prevent it from pivoting out with Volt Switch, so switch-ins to these Ground-types like Skarmory, Moltres, and Zapdos are appreciated.

Iron Hands.comments:
Other Options

An offensive set with Surge Surfer and Swords Dance can be utilized with a partner that can set up Electric Terrain like Hadron Engine Gholdengo and Latios, but this is a niche set that struggles to find success due to Electric Terrain only lasting a short amount of time and Iron Hands still being outsped by threats like Choice Scarf Latios, Deoxys-S, and Choice Scarf Scream Tail. Iron Hands can utilize a physically defensive Swords Dance set with Regenerator to check physical attackers like Chien-Pao and Roaring Moon while being quite threatening.

Checks and Counters

Psychic-types: Deoxys-S, Latios, and Azelf all threaten Iron Hands with their STAB moves.

Ground-types: Ground-types like Great Tusk and the Landorus formes wall Iron Hands and threaten it with Earthquake if it lacks Earth Eater.

Fairy-types: Scream Tail and Primarina frighten Iron Hands with their STAB moves, with Scream Tail, in particular, being able to bypass Substitute on Swords Dance Iron Hands thanks to Boomburst.

Swords Dance.description:
Amazing stats, solid typing, and access to Swords Dance allow Iron Hands to be a dangerous setup sweeper. Earth Eater lets Iron Hands set up safely versus Great Tusk, Swampert, and the Landorus formes as well as check Earthquake Roaring Moon. Substitute gives Iron Hands a way to set up freely on walls like Corviknight, Skarmory, and Iron Treads while preventing threats like Deoxys-S and Choice Band Zamazenta from coming in and forcing it out. 60 HP and 252 Special Defense EVs let the Substitute withstand two hits from Pecharunt's Malignant Chain, protecting Iron Hands from being poisoned.

Iron Hands is best suited for balance and bulky offense team structures that appreciate its defensive prowess and ability to sweep opposing teams. Scream Tail is a significant threat to Iron Hands, as it outspeeds and OHKOes Iron Hands with Pixilate Boomburst whether Iron Hands is behind a Substitute or not, so checks to it like Iron Treads, Heatran, and Iron Moth are important. Without a Substitute, Iron Hands is susceptible to strong attackers like Deoxys-S, Latios, and Azelf, so partners that can switch into these threats like the aforementioned Iron Treads as well as RegenVest Pokemon like Manaphy and Swampert are valuable; in return, they appreciate Iron Hands for its ability to check Roaring Moon, Great Tusk, and Kingambit. Iron Hands struggles to deal with Pecharunt, so offensive checks to it like Gholdengo and Roaring Moon are quite helpful.

RegenVest.description:
Iron Hands's high natural bulk, solid defensive typing, and access to Volt Switch allow it to be a great RegenVest user. Close Combat provides a strong Fighting-type STAB move that can potentially 2HKO RegenVest Swampert, whereas Drain Punch provides a bit of immediate recovery and doesn't hinder its bulk. Earthquake hits Pecharunt and Gholdengo. Ice Punch nails Ground-types that can block Volt Switch like Great Tusk and the Landorus formes. The given EV spread enables Iron Hands to check physical attackers like Chien-Pao and non-Choice Band Zamazenta, since it is unable to be 2HKOed by either at full HP, while also dealing with special attackers like Gholdengo, Zapdos, and Iron Moth.

Iron Hands is best suited for balance and offensive team structures that appreciate its defensive prowess and solid role compression in addition to its ability to scare many defensive Pokemon. Iron Hands is a good choice for teams that lack checks for physical Dark-types like Roaring Moon, Kingambit, and Chien-Pao as well as special attackers like Gholdengo and Iron Moth. Iron Hands struggles with Psychic- and Fairy-types like Latios, Deoxys-S, and Scream Tail; therefore, it appreciates allies like Iron Treads, Heatran, and Scream Tail. Ground-types like Great Tusk, Swampert, and the Landorus formes all threaten Iron Hands with their STAB moves and are immune to Volt Switch, so it finds partners such as Corviknight, Moltres, and Earth Eater Gholdengo valuable. Strong physical attackers like Cinderace, Choice Band Zamazenta, and the Ogerpon formes frighten Iron Hands; thus, physical walls like Pecharunt and the aforementioned Corviknight can help Iron Hands against these threats.

Surge Surfer.description: Surge Surfer Iron Hands is a great wallbreaker, leveraging the Speed boost procured by its ability to outspeed almost the entire unboosted metagame and set up with Swords Dance in order to break though opposing teams. It also has a great matchup against a lot of defensive staples such as Corviknight and Gholdengo. Ice Punch allows Iron Hands to hit Garchomp and foes that resist its STAB combination such as Latios, Dragonite, and Landorus-T for greater damage. Since Iron Hands is reliant on Electric Terrain to function, it must be paired with a Hadron Engine pivot such as Latios or Sandy Shocks, both of which can apply additional pressure on Great Tusk; in return, Iron Hands can beat Pokemon that Sandy Shocks is unable to hit such as Landorus-T. Iron Hands is not a conventional wallbreaker, and it can struggle to power through teams on its own; therefore, teammates such as Roaring Moon and Iron Boulder are appreciated, as they can apply pressure on Pokemon such as Deoxys-D and physically defensive Great Tusk, respectively, which Iron Hands struggles against.
